description: Daily market data pre-fetch cron job, 7-day chart viewing on 1h interval, and persistent drawing tools (horizontal/vertical lines) on trade charts

userStories.title: Daily Market Data Pre-Fetch Scheduled Task
userStories.description: As a system, I want to automatically fetch market data at 1:00 AM UTC daily for all symbols that exist in the trades table, so that chart data is always available even when no one imports trades.
acceptanceCriteria: New Trigger.dev scheduled task in src/trigger/daily-market-data-prefetch.ts using schedules.task() with cron 0 1 * * *
Queries trades table for distinct symbols across all users (SELECT DISTINCT symbol FROM trades WHERE symbol IS NOT NULL)
For each symbol, fetches previous day's (UTC) data using existing getOHLCBars() for both 1min and 1h base intervals — same code path as batch import MAE/MFE processing
Fetches every calendar day including weekends — Databento returns empty/error for non-trading days, some futures like bitcoin trade weekends
Per-symbol error handling: retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts), log failure, continue with remaining symbols
Concurrency limited to avoid Databento API rate limits (reuse concurrencyLimit: 10 pattern from processTradeMAEMFE)
Logs summary at completion: total symbols, successes, failures, skipped (already cached)
Core logic (symbol discovery + fetch orchestration) extracted into a testable function separate from Trigger.dev task wrapper
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)

userStories.title: Integration Tests for Daily Market Data Pre-Fetch
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests for the daily pre-fetch logic so that we can verify correct symbol discovery and error handling.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file created: tests/integration/daily-market-data-prefetch.test.ts
Tests distinct symbol extraction from trades table (inserts test trades with various symbols, verifies correct distinct list)
Tests that the pre-fetch function handles empty symbol list (no trades in DB) gracefully
Tests error handling when a symbol fetch fails (verifies it continues with others and reports failures)
Tests that both 1min and 1h intervals are requested per symbol
Uses setupTrader() or setupTraderWithTrades() fixtures for DB setup
All tests pass (bun run test)
Typecheck passes (bun run check)

userStories.title: Extended Chart Endpoint for 7 Trading Sessions
userStories.description: As a trader, I want a backend endpoint that returns up to 7 trading sessions of 1h bars so that I can see broader market context around my trades.
acceptanceCriteria: New tRPC endpoint marketData.getExtendedChartData that fetches 1h bars for an extended date range
Date range: 3 calendar days before entry through 3 calendar days after exit (or up to today) — ~7 trading sessions of context
Input: symbol, entryTime, exitTime (optional) — same pattern as getFullDayChartData
Uses existing getOHLCBars(symbol, 1h, date) for each day in range — same cache-first logic, no new Databento code
Returns bars in lightweight-charts format (timestamps in seconds), same response shape as getFullDayChartData
Data quality correctly aggregated across all fetched days (full/partial/unavailable)
Weekend/holiday days with no data naturally have no bars (no special handling needed)
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)

userStories.title: Chart Component 7-Day View on 1h Interval
userStories.description: As a trader, I want the trade detail chart to show 7 trading sessions when I select the 1h timeframe, while sub-hourly intervals keep showing trade day(s) only.
acceptanceCriteria: Chart component (tradingview-chart.tsx) calls getExtendedChartData when interval is 1h
Chart component calls existing getFullDayChartData for sub-hourly intervals (1min, 5min, 15min, 30min) — no changes to sub-hourly behavior
Two separate tRPC queries — no conditional logic inside a single query. The useMemo aggregation logic for sub-hourly intervals stays unchanged
When switching to 1h, chart re-fetches with the extended endpoint; switching back to sub-hourly uses existing 1min endpoint with client-side aggregation
Auto-fit recalculates visible range when switching between 1h (wide) and sub-hourly (narrow) to keep trade centered
Loading state shown during endpoint switch
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
Verify in browser: 1h shows ~7 days of context, sub-hourly shows trade day(s) only

userStories.title: Chart Annotations Schema and Backend CRUD
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a database schema and tRPC endpoints for chart drawing annotations so that horizontal and vertical lines persist per-trade across sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: New chart_annotations table in src/server/db/schema.ts with: id (text PK, ids.chartAnnotation()), tradeId (text FK to trades, onDelete cascade), userId (text FK to users, onDelete cascade), type (pgEnum: horizontal, vertical), value (decimal — price for horizontal, Unix timestamp seconds for vertical), lineStyle (pgEnum: solid, dashed), color (text, default #d4ff00), createdAt (timestamp with timezone)
Add chartAnnotation: () => createId('ca') to the ids map in src/lib/shared/id.ts
New tRPC router chartAnnotations with: list query (returns annotations for tradeId, validates user owns trade), create mutation, delete mutation (by ID), clearAll mutation (by tradeId)
All procedures use protectedProcedure with user ownership validation
Index on tradeId for fast lookups
Schema pushed with bun run db:push
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)

userStories.title: Integration Tests for Chart Annotations
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests for the chart annotations endpoints so that we can verify CRUD operations and ownership validation.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file created: tests/integration/chart-annotations.test.ts
Tests create mutation: creates horizontal and vertical annotations with correct values
Tests list query: returns annotations for a specific trade, empty for trades with none
Tests delete mutation: removes a single annotation by ID
Tests clearAll mutation: removes all annotations for a trade
Tests ownership validation: rejects create/delete/list/clearAll when user does not own the trade
Uses setupTraderWithTrades() fixtures
All tests pass (bun run test)
Typecheck passes (bun run check)

userStories.title: Chart Drawing Toolbar and Horizontal Line Tool
userStories.description: As a trader, I want a drawing toolbar on the chart with a horizontal line tool so that I can mark key price levels on my trade charts, with lines persisted to the database.
acceptanceCriteria: Drawing toolbar integrated into existing chart overlay controls bar (same row as symbol badge, timeframe selector, Fit button — positioned after Fit button)
Toolbar buttons follow existing chart button styling exactly: bg-muted px-2 py-1 font-mono text-[10px] rounded min-h-[36px] min-w-[36px] with hover:bg-muted/80
Buttons: Horizontal Line (Minus icon from lucide-react), Clear All (Trash2 icon). Vertical line button rendered but disabled (placeholder for US-008)
Active tool highlighted with bg-primary text-primary-foreground (same as active timeframe button pattern)
Click same tool again to deactivate (return to default crosshair/copy mode)
Line style toggle button cycling between solid/dashed

When horizontal tool is active: chart clicks read OHLC-snapped price from currentSnappedPriceRef and call seriesRef.current.createPriceLine() — same API used for SL/TP/MAE/MFE lines (lines 439-493 of tradingview-chart.tsx)
When horizontal tool is active: chart clicks save annotation to DB via chartAnnotations.create mutation
When no drawing tool is active: existing click-to-copy-price behavior preserved unchanged — wrap existing subscribeClick handler in conditional using activeDrawingToolRef
Drawing mode changes cursor to crosshair via CSS on chart container
Line color: default #d4ff00, small palette cycling button: #d4ff00, #00d4ff, #ff3b3b, #00ff88, #71717a
Clear All button calls chartAnnotations.clearAll and removes all user-drawn price lines from seriesRef
On chart mount: fetch annotations via chartAnnotations.list query and render horizontal annotations as price lines
Keyboard shortcuts: H to activate horizontal tool, Escape to deactivate, only when chart container is focused
Mobile: toolbar buttons use min-h-[36px] min-w-[36px] touch targets matching existing Fit button mobile pattern
All new UI elements have data-testid attributes
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
Verify in browser: place horizontal lines, switch styles/colors, delete via Clear All, refresh page and see lines persist

userStories.title: Vertical Line Drawing Tool
userStories.description: As a trader, I want a vertical line drawing tool so that I can mark key time points on my trade charts, with lines persisted to the database.
acceptanceCriteria: Vertical Line button (SeparatorVertical or GripVertical icon from lucide-react) enabled in the drawing toolbar (was disabled placeholder in US-007)

Vertical line implementation uses lightweight-charts ISeriesPrimitive interface: implement paneViews() returning a renderer that draws a vertical line via canvas drawLine(x, 0, x, height) on the scope provided by IPrimitivePaneRenderer.draw(target)
Primitive attaches via seriesRef.current.attachPrimitive(verticalLinePrimitive) and detaches via detachPrimitive()
When vertical tool is active: chart clicks read crosshair timestamp from param.time (stored in a ref by subscribeCrosshairMove) and create a vertical line primitive at that time coordinate
When vertical tool is active: chart clicks save annotation to DB via chartAnnotations.create mutation with type vertical and value as Unix timestamp in seconds
Vertical lines respect selected line style (solid/dashed) and color from toolbar state
Vertical lines render with axis label at bottom showing time
On chart mount: fetch annotations via chartAnnotations.list and render vertical annotations as ISeriesPrimitive instances
Clear All button also removes all vertical line primitives (detachPrimitive for each)
Keyboard shortcut: V to activate vertical tool (only when chart focused)
All new UI elements have data-testid attributes
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
Verify in browser: place vertical lines, switch styles/colors, clear all removes them, refresh page and see lines persist

userStories.title: E2E Tests for Chart Tools and 7-Day View
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests for the chart drawing tools and extended date range so that we can verify the UI works correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file created: tests/e2e/chart-tools.spec.ts
Tests that drawing toolbar renders on trade detail chart page
Tests horizontal line placement: activate tool, click chart, verify price line appears
Tests vertical line placement: activate tool, click chart, verify vertical line appears
Tests clear all: place lines, click clear, verify lines removed
Tests persistence: place line, navigate away, return, verify line still present
Tests that 1h interval shows extended date range (more bars visible than sub-hourly)
All new UI elements have data-testid attributes
All tests pass (bun run test:e2e)
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
